text: Summary Radical cross-coupling represents a new paradigm for forming C( sp 3 )–C and C( sp 3 )–heteroatom bonds using transition metals in conjunction with photoredox and/or electrochemical activation, as well as post-transition metals and small molecules. Only a few reports, mostly focused on silicon and germanium, have explored beyond transition metals. Here, we present the discovery that a low-valent bismuth complex can emulate the one-electron oxidative addition mode of common first-row transition metals with redox-active alkyl radical precursors. The resultant Bi–alkyl species undergo distinct transformations depending on the nature of the alkyl radical α-substituent. Elaboration of this reactivity mode led to the development of a bismuth-catalysed Cross-Coupling of C( sp 3 )-Radicals with Amides (BISCARAMIDES), a C( sp 3 )–N cross-coupling protocol operating under thermostable, ambient-pressure conditions, compatible with a range of synthetically relevant five-membered NH heterocycles.
